---
title: "Supporting Information 3"
author: "Daniel Y.M. Nakamura, Ward C. Wheeler, Taran Grant"
date: "2025-09-14"
output: html_document
---

Phylogenetic analyses of extant taxa have relied mostly on DNA sequences. The proportion of phenomic characters relative to molecular characters has been reduced with the advent of massive parallel DNA sequencing. However, phenomic evidence enables sampling taxa without molecular data (such as fossils). Here we examine the impact of phenomic evidence on phylogenetic analyses dominated by DNA sequences. We answer five questions using 57 empirical data sets (22 with morphology-only taxa). First, are topologies impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence in TE trees relative to MOL trees? Second, are distances between MOL and TE topologies associated with the proportion of phenomic characters? Third, is the addition of phenomic evidence equally impactful on ML and MP topologies? Fourth, how do bootstrap values of shared clades differ between MOL and TE trees? Fifth, can we retrodict the occurrence (i.e. monophyly) of TE groups based on bootstrap values of MOL clades? These questions were assessed with and without fossils.

# PRE-AMBLE

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Set the working directory 
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/")

# Seed
set.seed(44)

# Load libraries
library(ape)
library(cowplot)
library(dplyr)
library(dunn.test)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggridges)
library(glmmTMB)
library(gridExtra)
library(phytools)
library(RNODE)
library(rstatix)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(TreeDist)
library(viridis)
```

# Q1. Are topologies impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence?

Dataset with MOL-TE topological distances were calculated in script 1.

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")

print("EXTANT: Number of datasets impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (number)")
sum(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)")
sum(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)")
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)")
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC")
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)")
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 

print("EXTINCT: Number of datasets impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (number)")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)")
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)")
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC")
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)")
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
```

Considering extant taxa only (n = 57), the phenomic evidence impacted topologies in 87.7% (50/57) of the datasets in MP and ML (MKv with bias correction) and 85.9% (49/57) in ML (MK without bias correction). Considering both extant and extinct taxa (n = 22), the phenomic evidence impacted topologies in 95.5% (21/22) of the data sets and 81.8% (18/22) in ML (both in MKv and MK).

## Q1.1 Data exploration

### Q1.1.1 Extant (Table 1)

Considering extant taxa (n = 57; see Table 1):

```{r}
print("1.a MP MOL vs MP TE")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
sd(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
range(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
print("RF")
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
print("Mean SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
print("Min SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
print("Resolution MOL")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
print("Resolution TE")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE))

cat("\n1.b ML MOL vs ML TE MKv")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
print("RF")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
print("SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))

cat("\n1.c MLmol_MLteNoAsc")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
print("RF")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
print("SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
```

### Q1.1.2 Extant + Extinct (Table 1)

Considering both extant and extinct taxa (n = 22; see Table S4):

```{r}
print("1.a MP MOL vs MP TE")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
range(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
sd(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
range(df_extinct$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
print("RF")
mean(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
print("Mean SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
print("Min SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
print("Resolution MOL")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
print("Resolution TE")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE))

cat("\n1.b ML MOL vs ML TE MKv")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_nUniqueCladesTEASC)
print("RF")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
print("SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))

cat("\n1.c MLmol_MLteNoAsc")
print("No. shared clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nSharedClades)
print("No. unique MOL clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesMOL)
print("No. unique TE clades")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_nUniqueCladesTEnoASC)
print("RF")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
print("CID")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
range(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
print("SPR")
mean(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
sd(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
range(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))
```

## Q1.2 Statistical tests

### Q1.2.1 Extant (Table S4)

Considering extant taxa only, there are 50 datasets in which MOL-TE incongruences are not equal to zero and 7 datasets in which they are equal to zero in MP and ML (with MKv). In ML (with MK, no bias correction), there are 49 datasets in which MOL-TE incongruences are not equal to zero and 8 datasets in which they are equal to zero. To test if MOL-TE incongruences are significantly different from zero, we can check if the distribution of observed values are significantly different from zero. 

For each comparison and for each topological distance metric, we tested for normality. Because data are not normal according to Shapiro-Wilk tests (P < 0.05 in all cases), we tested if the median of each topological distance metric is different from zero using a non-parametric Wilcoxon Signed-Ranked test. First, the V statistic is computed by summing the signed ranks of the differences between the observed values and the hypothesized value (i.e. zero). The ranks are assigned based on the absolute values of the differences, and the sign is determined by whether the original value is positive or negative. Second, given that the distribution of V values is normally distributed for a large sample size due to the Central Limit Theorem, P-values can be calculated by finding the area under the normal curve corresponding to the calculated Z-score.

```{r}
print("1a. MPmol vs MPte")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))  # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_spr

print("1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1b_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1b_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1b_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_spr

print("1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_spr
```
In all cases, the median of normalized RF, CID, and SPR values in MP and ML are significantly different from zero. Thus, MOL-TE incongruences different from zero indicate that the phenomic evidence tends to impact topologies in empirical studies.

### Q1.2.2 Extant + extinct (Table S5)

Now, let's test the same for the 22 data sets including fossils.

```{r}
print("1a. MPmol vs MPte")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))  # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1a_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_spr

print("1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1b_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1b_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # parametric, p > 0.05
q1b_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1b_spr

print("1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc")
print("RF")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_rf
print("CID")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_cid
print("SPR")
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
q1c_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1c_spr
```


## Q1.3 Figures

### Q1.3.1 Extant (Figure S1)

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create a dataframe
df_extant <- data.frame(
  RFdist = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF),  # Combine the vectors into a single column
  CID = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID),
  SPR = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR),
  comparison = rep(c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"), each = length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))  # Repeat vector names
)
df_extant$comparison <- factor(df_extant$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999",
                    "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4",
                    "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)
s1_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=RFdist, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized RF", y=NULL,tag="A")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=CID, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized CID", y=NULL,tag="B")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=SPR, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized SPR", y=NULL,tag="C")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = comparison, y = CID, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
legend_grob <- cowplot::get_legend(legend)

#tiff("Figures/figS1_density.tiff", width = 6, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(s1_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=1),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
#dev.off()
```

### Q1.3.2 Extant + Extinct (Figure S2)

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create a dataframe
df_extinct <- data.frame(
  RFdist = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF),  # Combine the vectors into a single column
  CID = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID),
  SPR = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR),
  comparison = rep(c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"), each = length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))  # Repeat vector names
)
df_extinct$comparison <- factor(df_extinct$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999",
                    "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4",
                    "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)
s1_rf = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=RFdist, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized RF", y=NULL,tag="A")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_cid = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=CID, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized CID", y=NULL,tag="B")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_spr = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=SPR, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized SPR", y=NULL,tag="C")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = comparison, y = CID, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
legend_grob <- cowplot::get_legend(legend)

#tiff("Figures/figS2_density.tiff", width = 6, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(s1_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=1),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
#dev.off()
```


# Q2.Are MOL-TE incongruences associated with the proportion of phenomic characters? 

## Q2.1 Spearman correlations

To test if the proportion of phenomic characters is associated with the topological metrics of MOL-TE incongruences, we used a non-parametric Spearman's Rank Correlation test. The rho values measure the association between two variables based on their rank orders: a rho of +1 indicates a perfect monotonic increasing relationship; a rho of -1 indicates a perfect monotonic decreasing relationship; a rho of zero indicates a no monotonic relationship.

### Q2.1.1 Extant (Table 2)

```{r, warning=F}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("RF: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized RF\n", x = NULL)

print("CID: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized CID\n", x = NULL)

print("SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.33) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized SPR\n", x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

```{r, warning=F}
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.515) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles
  
print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.334) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

```{r, warning=F}
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.525) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.085, label.y = 0.42) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

### Q2.1.2 Extant + extinct (Table 2)

To test if the proportion of phenomic characters is associated with the topological metrics of MOL-TE incongruences, we used a non-parametric Spearman's Rank Correlation test. The rho values measure the association between two variables based on their rank orders: a rho of +1 indicates a perfect monotonic increasing relationship; a rho of -1 indicates a perfect monotonic decreasing relationship; a rho of zero indicates a no monotonic relationship.

```{r, warning=F}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("RF: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_rf_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized RF\n", x = NULL)

print("CID: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_cid_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized CID\n", x = NULL)

print("SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mp_spr_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.33) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized SPR\n", x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

```{r, warning=F}
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_rf_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_cid_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
 # stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.515) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles
  
print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mlasc_spr_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.334) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

```{r, warning=F}
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_rf_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_cid_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
 # stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.525) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
plot_mlno_spr_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
 # stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.085, label.y = 0.42) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
```

## Q2.2 Figures

### Q2.2.1 Extant (Figure 3)

```{r}
# Grid 3x3
tiff("Figures/fig3_proportion.tiff", width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
```

### Q2.2.2 Extant + extinct (Figure S3)

```{r}
# Grid 3x3
tiff("Figures/figS3_proportion_fossils.tiff", width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
```

# Q3. Is the addition of phenomic evidence equally impactful on ML and MP topologies?

## Q3.1 MOL-TE incongruences ~ Optimality criteria

To test whether the MOL-TE incongruences are different between ML and MP, we first need to change the structure of the dataframe.

### Q3.1.1 Extant (Table 3)

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create an empty data frame for RF
q3_rf <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of RF into the data frame
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF))))
# Summary of medians
q3_rf %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())

# Create an empty data frame for CID
q3_cid <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID))))
# Summary of medians
q3_cid %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())

# Create an empty data frame for SPR
q3_spr <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
# Summary of medians
q3_spr %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
```

Then, we need to statistically test whether MOL-TE incongruences (operationally measured as normalized RF, CID, and SPR) differ between (1) MP MOL vs TE, (2) ML MOL vs TE MKv (with bias correction), and (3) ML MOL vs TE MK (no bias correction). Because data are not normally distributed (Shapiro-Wilk tests: P < 0.05) and more than one category is present in the predictor variable, we performed the following tests:

- The Kruskal-Wallis test is a nonparametric test used to compare the medians (or ranks) between >2 groups. As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations must be independent. First, data is ranked (from the lowest to the highest value) and the mean rank computed. Next, we compute the statistic H (differences between group mean ranks and the overall mean rank, scaled by sample size). Finally, p-values can be estimated by comparing the statistic H and chi-squared distribution (if H is large, the p-value will be small).

- The Friedman test is a nonparametric test used to compare ordinal or continuous values that are paired between >2 groups. As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations can be dependent (paired). First, ranks are assigned to the pairwise structure across groups. Next, the average rank within group is computed. The Friedman statistic measures how far group rank sums deviate from the expected value in the null model. Finally, p-values are computed by comparing the Friedman statistic and chi-squared distribution.

- The Wilcoxon signed-rank test is a nonparametric test used to compare the ordinal or continuous values between 2 groups (e.g. as posthoc analysis of Friedman tests). As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations can be dependent. First, all values are ranked. The number of times that a value from one group precedes a value from the other is computed, generating the U statistic (the number of pairwise wins of one group over the other). Next, U is standardized to a normal distribution to obtain the p-value.


```{r, warning=FALSE}
print("RF")
shapiro.test(q3_rf$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)")
kruskal.test(q3_rf$value ~ q3_rf$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_rf_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_rf_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_rf_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

print("CID")
shapiro.test(q3_cid$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
kruskal.test(q3_cid$value ~ q3_cid$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_cid_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_cid_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_cid_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

print("SPR")
shapiro.test(q3_spr$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
kruskal.test(q3_spr$value ~ q3_spr$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_spr_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_spr_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_spr_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
```

### Q3.1.2 Extant + extinct (Table 3)

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create an empty data frame for RF
q3_rf_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of RF into the data frame
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF))))
# Summary of medians
q3_rf_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())

# Create an empty data frame for CID
q3_cid_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID))))
# Summary of medians
q3_cid_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())

# Create an empty data frame for SPR
q3_spr_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
# Summary of medians
q3_spr_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
```

```{r, warning=FALSE}
print("RF")
shapiro.test(q3_rf_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)")
kruskal.test(q3_rf_extinct$value ~ q3_rf_extinct$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_rf_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_rf_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_rf_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

print("CID")
shapiro.test(q3_cid_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
kruskal.test(q3_cid_extinct$value ~ q3_cid_extinct$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_cid_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_cid_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_cid_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

print("SPR")
shapiro.test(q3_spr_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
kruskal.test(q3_spr_extinct$value ~ q3_spr_extinct$source)
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
q3_spr_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_spr_fri))
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_spr_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
```

### Q3.1.3 Plot (Figure 4)

```{r}
# Set order of comparisons
q3_rf$source <- factor(q3_rf$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_cid$source <- factor(q3_cid$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_spr$source <- factor(q3_spr$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))

# Set colors
custom_palette <- c("MP_MOLvsTE" = "#339999", "ML_MOLvsTEASC" = "#6f77b4", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC" = "#cccccc")
# Create custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MP_MOLvsTE" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "ML_MOLvsTEASC" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)

# Boxplot Normalized RF
plot_q3_rf = ggplot(q3_rf, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "Normalized RF\n", tag="A") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot CID
plot_q3_cid = ggplot(q3_cid, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nNormalized CID\n", tag="B") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot SPR
plot_q3_spr = ggplot(q3_spr, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nSPR moves\n", tag="C") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none", axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.title = element_blank())+
#theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
```

```{r}
q3_rf_extinct$source <- factor(q3_rf_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_cid_extinct$source <- factor(q3_cid_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_spr_extinct$source <- factor(q3_spr_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))

# Boxplot Normalized RF
plot_q3_rf_extinct = ggplot(q3_rf_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="D") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot CID
plot_q3_cid_extinct = ggplot(q3_cid_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="E") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot SPR
plot_q3_spr_extinct = ggplot(q3_spr_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="F") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none", axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.title = element_blank())+
#theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
```

```{r}
# Plot 3x1
tiff("Figures/fig4_boxplots.tiff", width = 5, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(plot_q3_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_rf_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_cid_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_spr_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             nrow=3, ncol=2)
dev.off()
```


## Q3.2 Number of politomies

One importance difference, however, is the fact that trees were always binary in ML MOL and ML TE, whereas the addition of phenomic evidence can maintain, increase or decrease resolution of MP trees.

### Q3.2.1 Extant

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL")
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) > 0)
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE")
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) < 0)
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE")
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) == 0)
cat("\n")
print("MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies")
mean(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: SD no. polytomies")
sd(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: Min no. polytomies")
min(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: Max no. polytomies")
max(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP TE: Mean no. polytomies")
mean(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: SD no. polytomies")
sd(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: Min no. polytomies")
min(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: Max no. polytomies")
max(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
```

### Q3.2.2 Extant + extinct

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL")
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) > 0)
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE")
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) < 0)
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE")
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) == 0)
cat("\n")
print("MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies")
mean(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: SD no. polytomies")
sd(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: Min no. polytomies")
min(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP MOL: Max no. polytomies")
max(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("MP TE: Mean no. polytomies")
mean(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: SD no. polytomies")
sd(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: Min no. polytomies")
min(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("MP TE: Max no. polytomies")
max(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
```

### Q3.2.3 Statistical tests (Figure S4; Table S6)
Is the difference between MOL and TE resolution higher when fossils are incorporated? 

```{r}
# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
# 1. Filter rows where Dataset is duplicated
df_filtered <- df %>%
  filter(Dataset %in% Dataset[duplicated(Dataset)])
# 2. Keep only the relevant columns
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>%
  select(Type, MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE)
# 3. Compute paired difference
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>%
  mutate(diff = MP_nPolytomiesTE - MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
# 4. Reshape
df_extant <- df_filtered %>% filter(Type == "Extant")
df_extinct <- df_filtered %>% filter(Type == "Extinct")
n_extant <- nrow(df_extant)
df_extinct <- df_extinct %>% slice(1:n_extant)
df_extinct <- df_extinct %>%
  select(-Type) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_Extinct"))
df_wide <- cbind(df_extant, df_extinct)
df_wide <- df_wide %>% select(-Type)
# Exclude MP_nPolytomiesMOL_Extinct
df_reduced <- df_wide %>%
  select(-MP_nPolytomiesMOL_Extinct)

# 5. Exploratory (Table S5)
print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)

print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)

print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)

# 6. Statistical inference (Table S5)
df_friedman <- df_wide %>% 
  select(MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE, MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct)
# Friedman test
friedman_test <- friedman.test(as.matrix(df_friedman))
friedman_test
# Posthoc Wilcoxon
pairwise.wilcox.test(df_long$value, df_long$group, 
                     paired = TRUE, 
                     p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")

# 7. FIGURE S4
# Add row ID for pairing
df_plot <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())
# Convert to long format
df_long <- df_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE, MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct),
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "value"
  )
# Create a factor to order the groups in plotting
df_long$group <- factor(df_long$group, levels = c("MP_nPolytomiesMOL", 
                                                  "MP_nPolytomiesTE",
                                                  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct"))
# Plot paired dots with connecting lines
custom_colors <- c(
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE" = "#339999",         # lightest
  "MP_nPolytomiesMOL" = "#66CCCC",        # light
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct" = "#006666" # darker
)
# Custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MP_nPolytomiesMOL" = "MOL (without fossils)",
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE" = "TE (without fossils)",
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct" = "TE (with fossils)"
)
# Plot with gray lines, colored dots, renamed legend, no x-axis
p = ggplot(df_long, aes(x = group, y = value, group = row_id)) +
  geom_line(color = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = group), size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Polytomies\n", 
       title = "") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, labels = custom_labels, name=NULL)
p
# Save the plot as JPG, 300 dpi
ggsave(filename = "Figures/figS4_polytomies_MP.jpg", 
       plot = p, 
       width = 8, height = 5,      # size in inches
       dpi = 300,                   # resolution
       units = "in")
```


## Q3.3 Contribution of phenomic evidence to the TE optimality score

In addition to testing whether MOL-TE incongruences differ between optimaliy criteria, we can explore the impact of phenomic evidence to optimality scores between optimality criteria. Specifically, it is possible that the MKv model can constrain the contribution of the phenomic partition to the TE optimality score in ML, whereas the same does not occur in MP analyses. As such, we need to test whether the proportion of optimality scores between MORPH and TE is higher in MP than ML analyses. 

### Q3.3.1 Extant

```{r}
# Load data for extant taxa
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Exploratory data analyses
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC")
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC")
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)

print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC")
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC")
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)

print("Stats MP MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
sd(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
min(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
max(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)

print("Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
sd(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
min(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
max(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)

print("Stats ML MK MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
sd(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
min(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
max(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)

# Normality tests
shapiro.test(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric

# Friedman test (non-parametric, multiple groups, paired values)
q3_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_fri))

# Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

# Figure
values <- c(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
group <- c(rep("MP", length(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)),
             rep("ML MKV", length(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)),
             rep("ML MK", length(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)))
block = rep(1:length(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score), 3)  # Indices (same length as vectors)
df_nemenyi <- data.frame(value = values, group = group, block=block)
df_nemenyi$group <- factor(df_nemenyi$group, levels = c("MP", "ML MKV", "ML MK"))
custom_palette <- c("MP" = "#339999", "ML MKV" = "#6f77b4", "ML MK" = "#cccccc")

contribution_extant = ggplot(df_nemenyi, aes(x = group, y = value, group = block)) +
  #geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_line(aes(group = block), color = "black", alpha = 0.1) +  # Link paired values
  geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "% Phenomic/TE optimality score\n") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette)
```

### Q3.3.2 Extant + extinct

```{r}
# Load data for extant taxa
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Exploratory data analyses
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)

print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC")
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)

print("Stats MP MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
sd(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
min(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
max(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)

print("Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
min(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
max(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)

print("Stats ML MK MORPH/TE")
mean(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
sd(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
min(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
max(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)

# Normality tests
shapiro.test(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score) # p > 0.05, parametric
shapiro.test(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score) # p > 0.05, parametric

# Friedman test (non-parametric, multiple groups, paired values)
q3_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_fri))

# Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")

# Figure
values <- c(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
group <- c(rep("MP", length(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)),
             rep("ML MKV", length(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)),
             rep("ML MK", length(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)))
block = rep(1:length(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score), 3)  # Indices (same length as vectors)
df_nemenyi <- data.frame(value = values, group = group, block=block)
df_nemenyi$group <- factor(df_nemenyi$group, levels = c("MP", "ML MKV", "ML MK"))
custom_palette <- c("MP" = "#339999", "ML MKV" = "#6f77b4", "ML MK" = "#cccccc")

contribution_extinct = ggplot(df_nemenyi, aes(x = group, y = value, group = block)) +
  #geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_line(aes(group = block), color = "black", alpha = 0.1) +  # Link paired values
  geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "% Phenomic/TE optimality score\n") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette)
```

### Q3.3.3 Plot (Figure S5)

```{r}
s5= plot_grid(contribution_extant, 
          contribution_extinct, 
          labels = c("A", "B"),   # adds panel labels
          ncol = 1)               # A on top of B
# Save to JPG at 300 dpi
ggsave("Figures/figS5_contributionPhenomic.jpg", s5, dpi = 300,
       width = 6, height = 8, units = "in")  # adjust size
```


# Q4. How do bootstrap values of shared clades differ between MOL and TE trees? 

## Q4.1 Unique vs Shared (Table 1 and S7, Figure 6)

Before testing the differences of BS values in shared clades between MOL and TE trees, let's explore the differences of BS values between shared and unique clades.

First, we need to format the extant datasets.

```{r}
# Load shared clades
df_shared = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared.csv")
# Load unique clades
df_unique = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_unique.csv")

# Rename the column Analysis to Dataset
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Dataset = Analysis)
# Rename the column Method to Optimality
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Optimality = Method)
# Rename the column Type to Analysis
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Analysis = Type)
# Create a new column called Type (unique vs shared)
df_unique <- df_unique %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "unique")


# Create a new dataframe from df_shared with the selected columns
df_shared_stack <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "MOL",                 # new value
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_1,               # take Node_Tree_1
    Support = Support_Tree_1,         # take Support_Tree_1
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Bind the new rows below df_unique
df_unique_extended <- bind_rows(df_unique, df_shared_stack)
# Create a new dataframe from df_shared for Tree 2
df_shared_stack2 <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "TE",                  # new value for Tree 2
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_2,               # take Node_Tree_2
    Support = Support_Tree_2,         # take Support_Tree_2
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Append Tree 2 rows to df_unique_extended (which already contains Tree 1 and unique rows)
df_uniqueVSshared <- bind_rows(df_unique_extended, df_shared_stack2)

# Rename
df_uniqueVSshared <- df_uniqueVSshared %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML-ASC" = "ML_ASC",
      "ML-noASC" = "ML_noASC"
    )
  )
# Add the column Sampling (without fossils vs with fossils)
df_uniqueVSshared <- df_uniqueVSshared %>%
  mutate(Sampling = "Without_fossils")

View(df_uniqueVSshared)
```

Now, we need to format the datasets including fossils.

```{r}
# Load shared clades
df_shared = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared_extinct.csv")
# Load unique clades
df_unique = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_unique_extinct.csv")

# Rename the column Analysis to Dataset
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Dataset = Analysis)
# Rename the column Method to Optimality
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Optimality = Method)
# Rename the column Type to Analysis
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Analysis = Type)
# Create a new column called Type (unique vs shared)
df_unique <- df_unique %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "unique")

# Create a new dataframe from df_shared with the selected columns
df_shared_stack <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "MOL",                 # new value
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_1,               # take Node_Tree_1
    Support = Support_Tree_1,         # take Support_Tree_1
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Bind the new rows below df_unique
df_unique_extended <- bind_rows(df_unique, df_shared_stack)
# Create a new dataframe from df_shared for Tree 2
df_shared_stack2 <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "TE",                  # new value for Tree 2
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_2,               # take Node_Tree_2
    Support = Support_Tree_2,         # take Support_Tree_2
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Append Tree 2 rows to df_unique_extended (which already contains Tree 1 and unique rows)
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- bind_rows(df_unique_extended, df_shared_stack2)

# Rename
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- df_uniqueVSshared_extinct %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML-ASC" = "ML_ASC",
      "ML-noASC" = "ML_noASC"
    )
  )
# Add column Sampling (with vs without fossils)
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- df_uniqueVSshared_extinct %>%
  mutate(Sampling = "With_fossils")
View(df_uniqueVSshared_extinct)
```

The dataframes are formatted. Now, we can combine them, perform exploratory data analyses, and plot them.

```{r}
# Combine dataframes with and without fossils
df_combined <- bind_rows(df_combined, df_uniqueVSshared_extinct)
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(Optimality = recode(Optimality,
    "ML_ASC"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML MKv"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML MKV"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML_noASC" = "ML_MK",
    "ML MK"    = "ML_MK"
  ))
View(df_combined)

# Exploratory data analysis
q4_explore = df_combined %>%
  group_by(Analysis, Optimality, Type, Sampling) %>%
  summarise(
    min_Support = min(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_Support = max(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_Support = mean(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_Support = sd(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
View(q4_explore)

# Rename Optimality
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML_ASC" = "ML MKV",
      "ML_noASC" = "ML MK"
    )
  )

# Create a combined factor for coloring
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(
    ColorGroup = paste(Optimality, Type, Analysis, sep = "_")
  )

# common aesthetics / colors
fill_colors <- c("shared" = "#4d4d4d", "unique" = "#d9d9d9")

# Plot A: Without_fossils
pA <- df_combined %>%
  filter(Sampling == "Without_fossils") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Analysis, y = Support, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Optimality) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Bootstrap\n", fill = "Node type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Plot B: With_fossils
pB <- df_combined %>%
  filter(Sampling == "With_fossils") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Analysis, y = Support, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Optimality) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Bootstrap\n", fill = "Node type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
