```{r}
# Arrange side-by-side with labels A and B in upper-left
figure <- ggarrange(
  pA, pB,
  ncol = 1, nrow = 2,
  common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom",
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  label.x = 0.0,        # x position of labels (left)
  label.y = 0.98,        # y position of labels (top)
  font.label = list(size = 14)
)
figure
#ggsave("Figures/Fig6_BS_uniqueVSshared.jpg", plot = figure, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

We can statistically test whether bootstrap values differ between shared and unique clades, considering Type (categorical predictor: unique vs shared), Sampling (categorical predictor: Without_fossils vs With_fossils), Optimality (categorical predictor: MP vs ML_ASC vs ML_noASC), Analysis (categorical predictor: MOL vs TE). We will use both traditional non-parametric tests and GLMs.

```{r}
# Fix: Squeeze Support into (0,1) for GLMM with Beta distribution
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support + 0.5) / 101) 

# Split datasets
df_without <- df_combined %>% filter(Sampling == "Without_fossils")
df_with    <- df_combined %>% filter(Sampling == "With_fossils")

# --- Helper function for classical non-parametric tests ---
run_nonparametric_tests <- function(df) {
  results <- list()
  
  # 1. Kruskal-Wallis for Type, Optimality, Analysis
  results$kw_type <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Type)
  results$kw_opt  <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Optimality)
  results$kw_an   <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Analysis)
  
  # 2. Pairwise Wilcoxon for Type by Optimality & Analysis
  pw_list <- list()
  
  for(opt in unique(df$Optimality)){
    for(an in unique(df$Analysis)){
      subset_df <- df %>% filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == an)
      test_res <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
        subset_df,
        Support ~ Type,
        p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
      )
      test_res <- test_res %>% mutate(Optimality = opt, Analysis = an)
      pw_list[[paste(opt, an, sep = "_")]] <- test_res
    }
  }
  
  results$pairwise <- bind_rows(pw_list)
  return(results)
}

# Function to summarize mean, sd, min, max
summarize_support <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Optimality, Analysis, Type) %>%
    summarise(
      n    = n(),
      mean = mean(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd   = sd(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      min  = min(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      max  = max(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}


# All combinations to test
combinations <- expand.grid(
  Optimality = c("MP", "ML_MK", "ML_MKv"),
  Analysis = c("MOL", "TE"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to run pairwise GLMM for one subset
run_pairwise <- function(df_subset) {
  results <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(combinations)) {
    opt <- combinations$Optimality[i]
    ana <- combinations$Analysis[i]
    
    df_sub <- df_subset %>%
      filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == ana)
    
    if (nrow(df_sub) > 0) {
      # Fit beta GLMM
      glmm <- glmmTMB(
        Support_beta ~ Type + (1|Dataset),
        family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
        data = df_sub
      )
      
      # Pairwise comparisons for Type
      em <- emmeans(glmm, ~ Type)
      pw <- pairs(em, adjust = "bonferroni")
      
      results[[paste(opt, ana, sep = "_")]] <- summary(pw)
    }
  }
  
  bind_rows(results, .id = "Comparison")
}

run_pairwise_df <- function(df_subset, combinations_df, rand = "(1|Dataset)") {
  out_list <- list()
  for(i in seq_len(nrow(combinations_df))) {
    opt <- combinations_df$Optimality[i]
    ana <- combinations_df$Analysis[i]
    df_sub <- df_subset %>% filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == ana)
    
    # need at least two Type levels and some rows
    if(nrow(df_sub) < 4 || length(unique(df_sub$Type)) < 2) {
      warning(sprintf("Skipping %s_%s: insufficient data (n=%d, types=%s)",
                      opt, ana, nrow(df_sub), paste(unique(df_sub$Type), collapse=", ")))
      next
    }
    
    # Fit model safely
    glmm <- try(
      glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Type + (1|Dataset), data = df_sub, family = beta_family(link="logit")),
      silent = TRUE
    )
    if(inherits(glmm, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("Model failed for %s_%s: %s", opt, ana, attr(glmm, "condition")$message))
      next
    }
    
    # emmeans and pairwise contrasts
    em <- try(emmeans(glmm, ~ Type), silent = TRUE)
    if(inherits(em, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("emmeans failed for %s_%s", opt, ana))
      next
    }
    pw <- try(pairs(em, adjust = "bonferroni"), silent = TRUE)
    if(inherits(pw, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("pairs() failed for %s_%s", opt, ana))
      next
    }
    
    # convert to data.frame to get exact numeric p-values
    pw_df <- as.data.frame(pw)
    # add context columns
    pw_df <- pw_df %>%
      mutate(
        Optimality = opt,
        Analysis   = ana,
        Comparison = rownames(pw_df)  # usually "unique - shared" or similar
      ) %>%
      select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, everything())
    
    out_list[[paste(opt, ana, sep = "_")]] <- pw_df
  }
  
  if(length(out_list) == 0) return(tibble()) 
  res <- bind_rows(out_list, .id = "id")
  
  # Add nicely formatted p-values:
  res <- res %>%
    mutate(
      p.value_numeric = as.numeric(p.value),
      p.value_fmt = ifelse(
        is.na(p.value_numeric), NA,
        ifelse(p.value_numeric < 0.001,
               formatC(p.value_numeric, format = "e", digits = 2),           # scientific for <0.001
               formatC(p.value_numeric, format = "f", digits = 3))           # 3 decimals otherwise
    ))
  
  return(res)
}

```

```{r}
# Table S7

print("Mean, sd, and range of BS without fossils")
summary_without <- summarize_support(df_without)
summary_without

results_without <- run_nonparametric_tests(df_without)
print("Non-parametric Wilcoxon tests: Unique vs Shared BS (without fossils)")
results_without$pairwise

print("GLMM with Beta distribution (without fossils")
# Run for Without_fossils
pairwise_without_df <- run_pairwise_df(df_without, combinations)
# View results: numeric and formatted p-values
pairwise_without_df %>%
  select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, estimate, SE, df, p.value_fmt)
```

```{r}
# Table S7
print("Mean, sd, and range of BS with fossils")
summary_with <- summarize_support(df_with)
summary_with

print("Non-parametric Wilcoxon tests: Unique vs Shared BS (with fossils)")
results_with    <- run_nonparametric_tests(df_with)
results_with$pairwise 

print("GLMM with Beta distribution (without fossils")
# Run for Without_fossils
pairwise_with_df <- run_pairwise_df(df_with, combinations)
# View results: numeric and formatted p-values
pairwise_with_df %>%
  select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, estimate, SE, df, p.value_fmt)
```

As expected, bootstrap values of unique clades are statistically lower than those from shared clades.

## Q4.2 MOL vs TE (Table 4)

### Q4.2.1 Extant

First, we need to load the data. Support values of shared clades were identified and organized using the package RNODE. 

```{r}
# Load data (BS shared clades)
df_q4 = read.csv("Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared.csv")

# Set order of optimality criteria
df_q4$Optimality <- factor(df_q4$Optimality, levels = c("MP", "ML-ASC", "ML-noASC"))
df_q4$SupportDiff = df_q4$Support_Tree_1 - df_q4$Support_Tree_2
```

For each optimality criterion, we tested whether the paired values of bootstrap of shared clades differ between MOL and TE trees. First, we computed the number of clades in which bootstrap values were the same in MOL and TE trees, higher in MOL than TE trees, or higher in TE than MOL trees. Next, we tested the normality of bootstrap values using Shapiro-Wilk tests. Finally, we used both the non-parametric Wilcoxon signed-rank and Generalized Linear Mixed Models.

#### Q4.2.1.1 MP MOL vs MP TE

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MP <- df_q4[grepl("MP", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in MP MOL")
mean(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in MP MOL")
min(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in MP MOL")
max(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in MP TE")
mean(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in MP TE")
sd(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in MP TE")
min(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in MP TE")
max(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MP)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 == df_MP$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 > df_MP$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 < df_MP$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MP$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in MP MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MP$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in MP TE", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```

Data exploration of MP analyses revealed 47.9% (1093/2279) of the clades whose bootstrap values were the same between MOL and TE analyses, 19.5% (445/2279) of the clades whose bootstrap values were higher in MOL than TE analyses, and 32.5% (741/2279) of the clades whose bootstrap values were higher in TE than MOL analyses. Moreover, the bootstrap in MOL clades was 89.2 ± 14.2, whereas the boostrap in TE clades was 91.1 ± 13.6.

```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MP$Support_Tree_1),
                           as.numeric(df_MP$Support_Tree_2),
                           paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MP_glmm <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MP$Node_Tree_1, df_MP$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MP$Approach1, df_MP$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MP$Support_Tree_1, df_MP$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MP$ID, df_MP$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MP_glmm <- df_MP_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MP_glmm)
df_MP_glmm <- df_MP_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
mp_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MP_glmm)
summary(mp_beta)
```

#### Q4.2.1.2 ML MOL vs ML TE ASC

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MLASC <- df_q4[grepl("ML-ASC", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in ML MOL")
mean(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in ML MOL")
min(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in ML MOL")
max(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in ML TE ASC")
mean(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in ML TE ASC")
sd(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in ML TE ASC")
min(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in ML TE ASC")
max(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MLASC)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 == df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 > df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 < df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML TE MKv", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```


```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1),
                       as.numeric(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2),
                       paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MLASC_glmm <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MLASC$Node_Tree_1, df_MLASC$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MLASC$Approach1, df_MLASC$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1, df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MLASC$ID, df_MLASC$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MLASC_glmm <- df_MLASC_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MLASC_glmm)
df_MLASC_glmm <- df_MLASC_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
ml_asc_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MLASC_glmm)
summary(ml_asc_beta)
```

#### Q4.2.1.3 ML MOL vs ML TE noASC

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MLnoASC <- df_q4[grepl("ML-noASC", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in ML MOL")
mean(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in ML MOL")
min(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in ML MOL")
max(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in ML TE noASC")
mean(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in ML TE noASC")
sd(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in ML TE noASC")
min(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in ML TE noASC")
max(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MLnoASC)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 == df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 > df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 < df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML TE MKv", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```

```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1),
                       as.numeric(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2),
                       paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MLnoASC_glmm <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MLnoASC$Node_Tree_1, df_MLnoASC$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MLnoASC$Approach1, df_MLnoASC$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1, df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MLnoASC$ID, df_MLnoASC$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MLnoASC_glmm <- df_MLnoASC_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MLnoASC_glmm)
df_MLnoASC_glmm <- df_MLnoASC_glmm %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
ml_noasc_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MLnoASC_glmm)
summary(ml_noasc_beta)
```

### Q4.2.2 Extinct

First, we need to load the data including fossils. Support values of shared clades were identified and organized using the package RNODE. 

```{r}
# Load data (BS shared clades)
df_q4 = read.csv("Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared_extinct.csv")

# Set order of optimality criteria
df_q4$Optimality <- factor(df_q4$Optimality, levels = c("MP", "ML-ASC", "ML-noASC"))
df_q4$SupportDiff = df_q4$Support_Tree_1 - df_q4$Support_Tree_2
```

#### Q4.2.2.1 MP MOL vs MP TE

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MP <- df_q4[grepl("MP", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in MP MOL")
mean(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in MP MOL")
min(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in MP MOL")
max(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in MP TE")
mean(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in MP TE")
sd(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in MP TE")
min(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in MP TE")
max(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MP)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 == df_MP$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 > df_MP$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MP$Support_Tree_1 < df_MP$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MP$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in MP MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MP$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in MP TE", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```

```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MP$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MP$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MP$Support_Tree_1),
                           as.numeric(df_MP$Support_Tree_2),
                           paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MP_glmm_f <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MP$Node_Tree_1, df_MP$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MP$Approach1, df_MP$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MP$Support_Tree_1, df_MP$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MP$ID, df_MP$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MP_glmm_f <- df_MP_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MP_glmm_f)
df_MP_glmm_f <- df_MP_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
mp_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MP_glmm_f)
summary(mp_beta)
```

#### Q4.2.2.2 ML MOL vs ML TE ASC

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MLASC <- df_q4[grepl("ML-ASC", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in ML MOL")
mean(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in ML MOL")
min(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in ML MOL")
max(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in ML TE ASC")
mean(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in ML TE ASC")
sd(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in ML TE ASC")
min(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in ML TE ASC")
max(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MLASC)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 == df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 > df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1 < df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML TE MKv", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```

```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1),
                       as.numeric(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2),
                       paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MLASC_glmm_f <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MLASC$Node_Tree_1, df_MLASC$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MLASC$Approach1, df_MLASC$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MLASC$Support_Tree_1, df_MLASC$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MLASC$ID, df_MLASC$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MLASC_glmm_f <- df_MLASC_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MLASC_glmm_f)
df_MLASC_glmm_f <- df_MLASC_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
ml_asc_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MLASC_glmm_f)
summary(ml_asc_beta)
```

#### Q4.2.2.3 ML MOL vs ML TE noASC

```{r}
# Filter dataset keeping only MP
df_MLnoASC <- df_q4[grepl("ML-noASC", df_q4$Optimality), ]

# Exploratory data analysis
print("Mean BS in ML MOL")
mean(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Standard deviation BS in MP MOL")
sd(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Min BS in ML MOL")
min(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL
print("Max BS in ML MOL")
max(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # MOL

print("Mean BS in ML TE noASC")
mean(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Standard deviation BS in ML TE noASC")
sd(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Min BS in ML TE noASC")
min(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE
print("Max BS in ML TE noASC")
max(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # TE

print("Total no. shared clades between MOL and TE")
nrow(df_MLnoASC)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL == BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 == df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) 
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL > BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 > df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2)
print("No. shared clades with BS MOL < BS TE")
sum(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1 < df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) 

# Histograms
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))  # adjust margins
# Histogram MP MOL
hist(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML MOL", main="")
# A
mtext("A", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
# Histogram MP TE
hist(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2, breaks = 40, col = "lightblue", xlab = "BS in ML TE MKv", main="")
# B
mtext("B", side = 3, line = 0.5, adj = -0.1, font = 2)
```

```{r}
# Shapiro-Wilk test for normality
shapiro.test(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1) # P < 0.05, non-parametric
shapiro.test(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2) # P < 0.05, non-parametric

# Perform the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for paired values
wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1),
                       as.numeric(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2),
                       paired = TRUE)

# Dataset for GLMMs
df_MLnoASC_glmm_f <- data.frame(
  Node = c(df_MLnoASC$Node_Tree_1, df_MLnoASC$Node_Tree_2),
  Approach = c(df_MLnoASC$Approach1, df_MLnoASC$Approach2),
  Support = c(df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_1, df_MLnoASC$Support_Tree_2),
  ID = c(df_MLnoASC$ID, df_MLnoASC$ID)
)

# GLMM Beta
# Step 1. Scale Support from 0–100 to 0–1
df_MLnoASC_glmm_f <- df_MLnoASC_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_prop = Support / 100)
# Beta regression does not allow exact 0 or 1
n <- nrow(df_MLnoASC_glmm_f)
df_MLnoASC_glmm_f <- df_MLnoASC_glmm_f %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support_prop * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n)
ml_noasc_beta <- glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Approach + (1 | ID),
                 family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
                 data = df_MLnoASC_glmm_f)
summary(ml_noasc_beta)
```

## Q4.3 Plot (Figure 5)

First, we will plot the Figure 5A-C (without fossils).

```{r, warning=FALSE}
# Create a new column with the optimality criterion
df_MP_glmm$comparison = "MPmol_MPte"
df_MLASC_glmm$comparison = "MLmol_MLteAsc"
df_MLnoASC_glmm$comparison = "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"

# Merge all comparisons into the same dataset
df_plot = bind_rows(df_MP_glmm, df_MLASC_glmm, df_MLnoASC_glmm)
View(df_plot)

# Set order of comparisons
df_plot$comparison <- factor(df_plot$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
df_plot = df_plot[!is.na(df_plot$comparison), ]

# Set colors
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999", "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
# Create custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)

# Box Plot MP
plot_mp = ggplot(df_MP_glmm, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#339999", alpha = .04, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="A") +
  theme_minimal() +
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Box Plot ML ASC
plot_ml_asc = ggplot(df_MLASC_glmm, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#6f77b4", alpha = .04, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="B") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(50, NA)) + # Define o mínimo para 50, o máximo será ajustado automaticamente
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Box Plot ML noASC
plot_ml_noasc = ggplot(df_MLnoASC_glmm, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#cccccc", alpha = .07, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="C") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(50, NA)) + # Define o mínimo para 50, o máximo será ajustado automaticamente
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)

# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = comparison, y = Support, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
# Convert the plot to a grob
plot_grob <- ggplotGrob(legend)
# Extract the legend from the grob
legend_grob <- gtable::gtable_filter(plot_grob, "guide-box")

# Plot 2x2
jpeg("Figures/Fig5ABC.jpg", width = 2000, height = 1600, res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_ml_asc + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_ml_noasc + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=1, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
```

Second, we will plot the Figure 5D-E (with fossils).

```{r, warning=FALSE}
# Create a new column with the optimality criterion
df_MP_glmm_f$comparison = "MPmol_MPte"
df_MLASC_glmm_f$comparison = "MLmol_MLteAsc"
df_MLnoASC_glmm_f$comparison = "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"

# Merge all comparisons into the same dataset
df_plot = bind_rows(df_MP_glmm_f, df_MLASC_glmm_f, df_MLnoASC_glmm_f)
View(df_plot)

# Set order of comparisons
df_plot$comparison <- factor(df_plot$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
df_plot = df_plot[!is.na(df_plot$comparison), ]

# Set colors
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999", "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
# Create custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)

# Box Plot MP
plot_mp = ggplot(df_MP_glmm_f, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#339999", alpha = .04, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="D") +
  theme_minimal() +
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Box Plot ML ASC
plot_ml_asc = ggplot(df_MLASC_glmm_f, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#6f77b4", alpha = .04, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="E") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(50, NA)) + # Define o mínimo para 50, o máximo será ajustado automaticamente
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Box Plot ML noASC
plot_ml_noasc = ggplot(df_MLnoASC_glmm_f, aes(x = Approach, y = Support)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(color = "#cccccc", alpha = .07, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nBootstrap\n", tag="F") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(50, NA)) + # Define o mínimo para 50, o máximo será ajustado automaticamente
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)

# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = comparison, y = Support, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
# Convert the plot to a grob
plot_grob <- ggplotGrob(legend)
# Extract the legend from the grob
legend_grob <- gtable::gtable_filter(plot_grob, "guide-box")

# Plot 2x2
jpeg("Figures/Fig5DEF.jpg", width = 2000, height = 1600, res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_ml_asc + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_ml_noasc + theme(plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=1, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
```

# Q5. Can bootstrap from MOL clades retrodict the monophyly of TE groups?

The datasets used in the Q5 was prepared with the package RNODE using the script 3.

## Q5.1 Extant

```{r}
# Load data
df_q5 = read.csv("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q5_09-25.csv")
# Only keep >50% BS
df_q5 <- df_q5[df_q5$support_tree1 >= 50, ]

# Select MP 
MPlogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MP", ]
# Select ML ASC
MLASClogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MLASC", ]
# Select ML no ASC
MLnoASClogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MLnoASC", ]
```

### Q5.1.1 Data exploration (Table S8)

```{r}
#############################
# EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSES #
#############################

# A) MP
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MPlogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE

# B) ML ASC
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MLASClogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE


# C) ML noASC
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MLnoASClogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE

```

### Q5.1.2 Logistic regressions (Table 5)

```{r}

#######################
# LOGISTIC REGRESSION #
#######################

# A) MP
# Perform logistic regression
mp_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MPlogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mp_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mp_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mp_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.09 and exp(0.09) is 1.1, then each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 10%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MPlogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#339999", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "Bootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree") +
  theme_minimal()

# B) ML ASC
# Perform logistic regression
mlasc_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MLASClogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mlasc_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mlasc_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mlasc_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.06 and exp(0.09) is 1.06, then the each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 6%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MLASClogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#6f77b4", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "Bootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree") +
  theme_minimal()

# C) ML noASC
# Perform logistic regression
mlnoasc_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MLnoASClogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mlnoasc_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mlnoasc_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mlnoasc_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.06 and exp(0.09) is 1.06, then the each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 6%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MLnoASClogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#cccccc", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "\nBootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree\n") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Q5.2 Extinct

```{r}
# Load data
df_q5 = read.csv("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q5_09-25_extinct.csv")
# Only keep >50% BS
df_q5 <- df_q5[df_q5$support_tree1 >= 50, ]

# Select MP 
MPlogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MP", ]
# Select ML ASC
MLASClogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MLASC", ]
# Select ML no ASC
MLnoASClogistic <- df_q5[df_q5$optimality == "MLnoASC", ]
```

### Q5.2.1 Data exploration (Table S9)

```{r}
#############################
# EXPLORATORY DATA ANALYSES #
#############################

# A) MP
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MPlogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MPlogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MPlogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MPlogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE

# B) ML ASC
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MLASClogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLASClogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE


# C) ML noASC
# Total number of MOL clades
nrow(MLnoASClogistic)
# Shared clades
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of shared clades
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion shared/total
mean(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 1)$support_tree1) # BS range
# Unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of unique clades for MOL
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion uniqueMOL/total
mean(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS mean
sd(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS sd
range(subset(MLnoASClogistic, occurrence_tree2 == 0)$support_tree1) # BS range
# MOL clades with BS <75 present in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1)) # Number of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 < 75 & occurrence_tree2 == 1))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL clades with BS <75 that occur in TE
# MOL clades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # Number of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 > 90 & occurrence_tree2 == 0))/nrow(MLnoASClogistic) # Proportion of MOL lades with BS >90 absent in TE
nrow(subset(MLnoASClogistic, support_tree1 == 100 & occurrence_tree2 == 0)) # MOL clades with BS = 100 absent in TE

```

### Q5.2.2 Logistic regressions (Table 5)

```{r}
#######################
# LOGISTIC REGRESSION #
#######################

# A) MP
# Perform logistic regression
mp_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MPlogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mp_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mp_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mp_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.09 and exp(0.09) is 1.1, then each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 10%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MPlogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#339999", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "Bootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree") +
  theme_minimal()

# B) ML ASC
# Perform logistic regression
mlasc_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MLASClogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mlasc_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mlasc_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mlasc_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.06 and exp(0.09) is 1.06, then the each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 6%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MLASClogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#6f77b4", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "Bootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree") +
  theme_minimal()

# C) ML noASC
# Perform logistic regression
mlnoasc_logistic_result <- glm(occurrence_tree2 ~ support_tree1, data = MLnoASClogistic, family = binomial)
# Print a summary of the model
summary(mlnoasc_logistic_result)
# Calculate 95% confidence intervals for the coefficients
confint(mlnoasc_logistic_result)
# Interpretation
exp(mlnoasc_logistic_result$coefficients[[2]]) # If coefficient is 0.06 and exp(0.09) is 1.06, then the each unit of 1% bootstrap in MOL clade increases the chance of the occurrence of this clade in TE by 6%

# Plot the logistic regression curve with ggplot2
ggplot(MLnoASClogistic, aes(x = support_tree1, y = occurrence_tree2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Plot the data points
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              color = "#cccccc", size = 1.2) +  # Logistic regression curve
  labs(x = "\nBootstrap of MOL clade", y = "Probability of occurrence in the TE tree\n") +
  theme_minimal()
```
